license_policy.default_content_license: Original repo-authored course design; source text must use public-domain or explicitly licensed editions before inclusion.
license_policy.source_text_boundary: This metadata references great works but does not include copyrighted translations or copied course assets.

source_canon.author: Euclid
source_canon.role: Definitions, postulates, common notions, construction, demonstration, and cumulative proof craft.
source_canon.use_boundary: Reference and public-domain edition metadata only until source packets prove text rights.

source_canon.author: Aristotle
source_canon.role: Terms, predication, classification, and category-error detection.
source_canon.use_boundary: Use source references and original exercises unless a licensed translation is recorded.

source_canon.author: Aristotle
source_canon.role: Propositions, affirmation, denial, contradiction, and modal language.
source_canon.use_boundary: Use source references and original exercises unless a licensed translation is recorded.

source_canon.author: Aristotle
source_canon.role: Syllogistic reasoning, valid form, and inference discipline.
source_canon.use_boundary: Use source references and original exercises unless a licensed translation is recorded.

source_canon.author: Aristotle
source_canon.role: Demonstration, first principles, causes, and explanatory knowledge.
source_canon.use_boundary: Use source references and original exercises unless a licensed translation is recorded.

source_canon.author: Aristotle
source_canon.role: Dialectical questions, endoxa, objections, and ordered inquiry.
source_canon.use_boundary: Use source references and original exercises unless a licensed translation is recorded.

source_canon.author: Aristotle
source_canon.role: Fallacy detection, sophistry, and bad-faith argument repair.
source_canon.use_boundary: Use source references and original exercises unless a licensed translation is recorded.

source_canon.author: Aristotle
source_canon.role: Ethos, pathos, logos, audience, persuasion, and civic speech.
source_canon.use_boundary: Use source references and original exercises unless a licensed translation is recorded.

source_canon.author: Plato
source_canon.role: Socratic questioning, intellectual humility, and dialogue practice.
source_canon.use_boundary: Use public-domain editions or source references with edition metadata.

source_canon.author: Cicero and Quintilian
source_canon.role: Invention, arrangement, character of the speaker, eloquence, and public argument.
source_canon.use_boundary: Use licensed or public-domain editions only.

modules.title: Attention And Distinction
modules.assessment: Explain three ways one debate fails because categories were mixed.
modules.performance_pressure: Private written analysis and short spoken explanation.

modules.title: Terms, Definitions, And Categories
modules.assessment: Repair a confused argument by stabilizing its key terms.
modules.performance_pressure: Friendly questioner asks for examples and counterexamples.

modules.title: Propositions And Contradiction
modules.assessment: Map a paragraph into propositions and test consistency.
modules.performance_pressure: Confused listener asks whether two claims can both be true.

modules.title: Euclidean Proof Craft
modules.assessment: Reconstruct a simple proof-like argument from accepted premises.
modules.performance_pressure: Learner explains each step aloud without skipping warrant questions.

modules.title: Valid Inference And Syllogism
modules.assessment: Translate a public claim into syllogistic form and test validity.
modules.performance_pressure: Skeptical listener challenges validity and premise truth separately.

modules.title: Demonstration And First Principles
modules.assessment: Write a demonstration-style explanation of a real-world claim.
modules.performance_pressure: Skeptical listener asks what would have to be true first.

modules.title: Dialectic And Ordered Questions
modules.assessment: Conduct a written dialogue that clarifies disagreement.
modules.performance_pressure: Friendly opponent answers unpredictably but cooperatively.

modules.title: Fallacies And Sophistry
modules.assessment: Produce a fallacy repair packet for a combative argument.
modules.performance_pressure: Combative opponent uses insults, topic shifts, and false dilemmas.

modules.title: Rhetoric And Ethical Persuasion
modules.assessment: Deliver a short speech outline with ethical constraint notes.
modules.performance_pressure: Public audience needs clarity without oversimplification.

modules.title: Dialogue And Disagreement
modules.assessment: Steelman and answer a position the learner strongly rejects.
modules.performance_pressure: Skeptical listener challenges motive and conclusion.

modules.title: Modern Transfer
modules.assessment: Analyze a modern argument and identify what would change the conclusion.
modules.performance_pressure: Learner answers a fast-moving current-events claim without overclaiming.

modules.title: Capstone Performance
modules.assessment: Submit and defend the Clear Reasoning Capstone Portfolio.
modules.performance_pressure: Live disputation, public speech, and final self-correction.

required_artifacts: persuasive speech outline and performance rubric
capstone.passing_standard: The learner must demonstrate sound reasoning, fair representation, ethical persuasion, clear speech, and specific self-correction.
